name: S. boulardii for pediatric acute diarrhea (China)
summary: Across 10 randomized trials of Chinese children with acute diarrhea, adding S. boulardii CNCM I-745 to standard rehydration reduced treatment failure and increased cure rates, and was well tolerated.
description: A systematic review and meta-analysis (McFarland & Li 2025) of 10 RCTs (1,125 children) conducted in China, none previously included in English-language reviews. All trials gave conventional rehydration to both arms and used open (non-placebo) controls.
interpretation: All 10 trials were open-label with no placebo, single-country (China), and mostly moderate quality, and publication bias was detected — so the effect size is probably optimistic. The review also reported diarrhea about 1.6 days shorter with S. boulardii (a continuous measure not shown here) and fewer adverse events (RR 0.19). The study was funded by the manufacturer (Biocodex).
methodologyNotes: Data are the per-trial 2×2 counts from Table 1 of McFarland & Li 2025. Outcomes are shown as their complements (treatment failure, not cured) so that lower is better; the source review reported the success direction (total effectiveness RR 1.22, 95% CI 1.16-1.28; cure RR 1.47, 95% CI 1.30-1.67). Diarrhea duration (a standardized mean difference) and the pooled adverse-event result are described but not encoded as 2×2. Pooled here by random-effects; the review used Mantel-Haenszel, so values may differ slightly.

outcomes.label: Treatment failure (no improvement)
outcomes.description: No meaningful improvement in diarrhea by end of treatment (complement of the total effectiveness rating).

outcomes.label: Not cured by end of treatment
outcomes.description: Diarrhea not resolved by end of treatment (complement of cure).

studies.author: Qu
studies.citation: Qu YH. Clinical efficacy of Saccharomyces boulardii in the treatment of acute diarrhea in children. Contemp Medi Symp. 2012;10:211.
studies.endpointDefinition: No meaningful improvement by end of treatment (complement of total effectiveness rating).
